MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with Case No. PCN-17-301376; the Conservatorship of Felix Zamudio Juarez.

Appearances: Court-Appointed Counsel Franchesca Callejo appearing by Zoom for Felix Zamudio Juarez. Andrew Schultz appearing by Zoom for Andrea Leung. Esther Rodriguez (Daughter/Interested Party) also appeared.

The Court stated before it today is the Amended First Account and Report of Conservator And Petition for its Settlement and the First Account and Report of Successor Trustee and Petition for its Settlement. Mr. Schult stated that the petitions were briefed extensively, that they summarized, that it would not have been productive to go line by line, that the amount did decrease for the account period since the goals would not be reached, and that without drastic action that they tried to to address months where there was excessive time. Ms. Callejo stated that she had nothing to add, that her client had concerns, that she represented those concerns, that the transition has been successful, that she hasn't heard anything, and that she's hopeful that means things are less contentious. Ms. Rodriguez argued in support of Mr. Schultz and Ms. Leung's fees stating that they should be paid in full, that both of them have done so much to try and help the situation, that both of them went above and beyond noting that her Sister that lives with her Dad is difficult, that the Court should give them the full amount, and that her Sister Sandra Turner agrees. The Court stated that there are objections, that it went through all the time records, that it was in Dept. 514 and heard a few matters there, that it has a sense of the dynamics here, and that this is a line by line case. The Court further stated that it went through line by line, that the review/payment of invoices was done week by week which ended up with a trip to the bank, that it did consider entries for appearances in harrassment hearings, that it was not sure that the Conservator needed to attend those hearings, that it may reduce parts of those items, and that Mr. Juarez has a lot of people around him that love & care for him but there is conflict. At the conclusion of comments, the Court took the Amended First Account and Report Of Conservator And Petition For Its Settlement; Petition For Allowance Of Conservator's Fee And Attorneys' Fees And Costs; Petition For Waiver Of Future Accounting; And Petition To Discharge Surety And Dispense With Bond (in PCN-17-301376) and the First Account And Report of Successor Trustee And Petition For Its Settlement; Petition For Allowance Of Successor Trustee's Fees And Attorneys' Fees And Costs; And Petition To Reduce Bond (PTR-18-302022) under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-04-2020 Continued To Mar-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-17-301376 Conservatorship of Felix Zamudio Juarez and PTR-18-302022 The Felix Zamudio Juarez 2010 Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Andrea Leung (Conservator/Trustee).
Franchesca Callejo Court appointed Counsel for Felix Juarez (Conservatee).
Patricia Juarez (Daughter of Conservatee) in Propria Persona.
Ernesto Juarez (Son of Conservatee) also present.

The Court expressed concern about the amount of fees, stated that they are quite high, and that it understands there is an issue with family dynamics, but it does not know that it fully explains the reasonableness of the requested fees. Ms. Callejo stated that her Client's main worry was the amount of fees, that she has not gone through line by line with her Client, noted that there are $146,000.00 in fees and stated that she thinks her Client would like to go through the fee request. Ms. Callejo also asked the Court to consolidate this accounting with Ms. Leung's final accounting to have all of the fees before the Court, stated that the Son and Daughter have also expressed concern, and that the fees are high in proportion to what's in the liquid assets of the estate. In addition, Ms. Callejo stated that she is not aware of anyone having a problem with a Successor, that the Conservatee is very content in his home and with Patricia and Ernesto helping him, and that the other Children are not visiting of calling the Conservatee. Mr. Schultz stated that the other Children contacted him/Ms. Leung and told them that they are not able to reach their Father. Ms. Callejo stated that she has explained to the adult Children what they need to do to express their concerns and that she did not see anything alarming in the accountings. The Court stated that it may be that it needs to address this accounting prior to the final accounting, and that it may be able to bifurcate fees, and approve this accounting. Mr. Schultz stated that he has no concern with going with one way, or the other. Ms. Juarez stated that she would like to object to the fees, that the fees are the primary issue, that there are other things that are not being attended to which are not to her and her Father's satisfaction, that mail being sent to her is not being certified and that she would like to see proof of what's being sent to her. The Court explained that there is no requirement of certified mail and that it will not require more than what the code requires. Ms. Juarez stated that the fees are inflated and that it is very concerning to her and her Father. The Court reminds Ms. Juarez that Ms. Callejo represents her Father and stated that it does not want Ms. Juarez to represent her Father's concerns. The Amended First Account and Report of Conservator, etc. and the First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. are continued to March 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any objections are to be filed no later than February 21, 2020. The Court encourages Ms. Juarez to seek the assistance of a Lawyer to see what her rights are.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee And Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision
Off Calendar Nov-29-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for (Temporary Trustee) Andrea Leung. Court Appointed Counsel Francesca Callejo for (Settlor/Conservatee) Felix Juarez.

The Court asked if everyone was in agreement with the settlement or if there are objections. Mr. Schultz reported that everyone in attendance agreed and that there have been no objections. Ms. Callejo stated that all of the properties are problematic in regards to renter issues and squatters and that her client is in agreement with the sale of the properties. The Court asked how long the proceeds will keep the conservatee in his home. Ms. Callejo stated that it depends on the expenses of the conservatorship and trust, that they do not expect a lot of capital gains, that they expect a net of about $500,000.00, that the conservatee has a good monthly income and as long as he does not require additional caregiving he should be fine. The Court asked if bond remains at $62,000.00. Ms. Callejo stated that it is now until the properties sell. Mr. Schultz stated that they have the bond and will file it as soon as they get the order. The Court granted the petition to Appoint Successor Trustee and Bring trust Under Continuing Court Supervision (subject to review of the order). A Status on Filing Bond is set for November 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A Status on Filing the First Accounting is set for May 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
